patch_type: Clean TrustMap rebuild, kernel-centered enterprise map, zoom/pan, ring-layer model, scenario-aware trust distribution
release_chain_status: V54 makes TrustMap the active wow-factor center of CyberShield. It replaces legacy layer filters and process-flow behavior with a kernel-centered enterprise TrustMap, ring-layer model, pan/zoom controls, side metric panels, scenario-specific trust distributions, and three-layer demo depth. No new top-level tabs were added.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
version_label_rule: Build and version labels belong in Settings/admin metadata and repo documentation only. They must not appear as executive-facing dashboard content.
core_thesis: CyberShield evaluates whether the information behind a critical action can be trusted before the business acts.
core_question: Can we trust the information behind this action before the business acts?
trust_model_doctrine: Executives rarely have perfect information. CyberShield is not about perfect evidence. Evidence supports the Trust Model, but evidence volume is not the point. Information reliability, source confidence, owner accountability, verification path, and consequence if wrong are the point.
trustmap_doctrine: The CyberShield Core is the TrustMap kernel, not a normal node. TrustMap must behave like a kernel-centered enterprise map with ring-layer depth, zoom, pan, clickable domains, clickable assets, object detail, and trust-weighted connections. It must not regress into a left-to-right process flow or confusing layer filters.
v54_changes: legacy V53.1 map removed from active loader chain
TrustMap kernel centered on CyberShield Core
right trust distribution and trend panel

implemented_release_train.v52: Model Registry Foundation and Human-First Executive Control View
implemented_release_train.v52.1: TrustMap Visual Restoration and Layer Patch
implemented_release_train.v52.2: Guided Onboarding, Routed Dashboard, and Layered TrustMap Patch
implemented_release_train.v52.4: TrustMap Core Logo and Control Plane Anchor Patch
implemented_release_train.v52.7: TrustMap Navigation and Report Output System
implemented_release_train.v53: Trust Model and Deep Scenario Spine Build

prototype_boundary: The current public build is a static advisory prototype. It models trust recommendations, trust distributions, trend lines, and proof records but does not perform legal determinations, CMMC certification, healthcare compliance validation, production enforcement, live email delivery, CRM sync, or live enterprise integrations.
known_limitations: V54 is a clean TrustMap rebuild inside the existing app shell
TrustMap visual object art remains prototype-grade
scenario data should be moved to JSON or a dedicated registry
reports download as text files rather than branded PDFs
contact capture is client-side only and not stored
CMMC guidance is advisory and not a certification or legal determination
healthcare guidance is advisory and not compliance validation
scoring and trend lines are demo-directional and not statistically validated
hands-on Firefox, Brave, Android, and desktop QA still needs to be performed after GitHub Pages deploys
